text: AI is rapidly changing the workplace, bringing both opportunities and challenges. One major impact is automation, where machines handle repetitive tasks like data entry or assembly line work. This frees up employees to focus on more complex activities. Additionally, productivity tools powered by AI, such as smart scheduling software or virtual assistants, help workers manage their time more efficiently. However, rather than replacing humans entirely, many jobs now emphasize human-AI collaboration. For example, doctors use AI to analyze medical images, while making the final diagnosis themselves. As a result, job roles are evolving. Some old positions may disappear, but new ones—like AI trainers or ethics officers—are emerging. Adapting to these changes through learning is key.
